reasoning: The resolution criteria are extremely strict: the creator explicitly clarified in May 2025 that "Only a formally ratified peace treaty will count for a YES resolution," superseding the initial text that included an "armistice." While there has been a recent surge in diplomatic momentum—driven by U.S. pressure under Trump, leading to direct talks in Istanbul in May and June 2025—the two sides remain miles apart on core issues. Russia is demanding massive territorial concessions, demilitarization, and political shifts in Kyiv, while Ukraine demands a ceasefire and rejects territorial capitulation. Even if a sudden political breakthrough or ceasefire occurs in the next six months, translating that into a *formally ratified* peace treaty by December 31, 2025, is practically impossible. Ratification would likely require Ukraine to lift martial law, hold elections, and potentially amend its constitution to address territorial changes—processes that take many months. Factoring in the logistical timeline of treaty drafting and parliamentary ratification, alongside the entrenched maximalist demands of both Putin and Zelenskyy, the probability of fulfilling this exact strict criterion before 2026 is extremely low.
